mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 60% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 65% of high-inflation observations or Rank IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above threshold yields negative 60d returns in > 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment indicate a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 55% of instances or interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5% signal restrictive monetary conditions that increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%.
